articles.title: What is DigiDocs?
articles.summary: Overview of the DigiDocs digital work management system.
articles.content_md:
# What is the DigiDocs?

DigiDocs is the DigiDocs Demo Ltd digital work management app. It replaces paper forms and shared spreadsheets with a mobile-first web app for daily site and office workflows.

## What it covers

The modules you see depend on your role and permissions. Common areas include:

- Timesheets
- Van, plant, and HGV daily checks
- Projects documents and signatures
- Absence and leave
- Maintenance, fleet, workshop tasks, and inventory
- Approvals, reports, customers, quotes, toolbox talks, and admin tools

## Access

Open the app at https://digidocs.mpdee.co.uk and sign in with your work account.

The app works on desktop, tablet, and mobile. For regular phone or tablet use, install it from the Help page so it opens like a normal app.

## DigiDocs benefit

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

## Example

For example, the same record can be searched, reviewed, and reported on later instead of being lost in paper or chat messages.

# Understanding the Dashboard

The Dashboard is your home screen after signing in. It shows quick links for the modules you can access and hides modules that are not enabled for your current role or team.

## Quick actions

The large action cards open day-to-day modules such as Timesheets, Daily Checks, Projects, Absence, Maintenance, Fleet, Workshop, Inventory, and Help. If a module is not visible, your role does not currently have that permission.

Managers and admins may also see tools such as Approvals, Actions, Toolbox Talks, Reports, Suggestions, Customers, Quotes, User Management, Admin Settings, FAQ Editor, and Error Reports.

1. Open Safari and go to https://digidocs.mpdee.co.uk
2. Tap the **Share** button (square with arrow)

4. Tap **Add** in the top right

## Android (Chrome)

1. Open Chrome and go to https://digidocs.mpdee.co.uk
2. Tap the **three dots** menu in the top right
3. Tap **Add to Home screen**

Your role and module permissions decide what you can see and do in DigiDocs.

## Employee

Employees can use the day-to-day modules enabled for their role or team, such as Timesheets, Van Daily Checks, Plant Daily Checks, HGV Daily Checks, Projects, Absence, Maintenance, Workshop Tasks, Fleet, Inventory, or Help.

## Manager

Managers may also have access to approval and oversight tools, such as Approvals, Actions, Toolbox Talks, Reports, Suggestions, and selected admin workflows.

## Admin

Admins can manage system areas such as users, roles, permissions, customers, quotes, admin settings, FAQ content, fleet assets, and error reports, depending on the module permissions enabled.

articles.title: How do I create a new timesheet?
articles.summary: Step-by-step guide to creating and submitting a weekly timesheet.
articles.content_md:
# How do I create a new timesheet?

## Quick Steps

1. Go to **Timesheets** from the dashboard or navigation
2. Click the **New Timesheet** button
3. Select the **Week Ending** date (must be a Sunday)
4. The system checks if a timesheet already exists for that week
5. Fill in each day's hours using the day tabs (Mon-Sun)
6. Add your digital signature
7. Click **Submit**

## Week Selector

Before entering data, you'll see the Week Selector:
- Choose which Sunday ends your timesheet week
- Only Sundays can be selected
- If a timesheet already exists for that week, you'll be prompted to edit it instead

## Entering Daily Hours

For each day:
1. Click the day tab (Mon, Tue, etc.)
2. Enter **Start Time** and **End Time**
3. Hours are calculated automatically
4. Optionally add a **Job Number**
5. Add any **Remarks** if needed

## Special Options

- **In Yard** - Mark if you worked in the yard that day
- **Did Not Work** - Mark days you didn't work (sick, leave, weekend, etc.)

## Important Rules

- All 7 days must have hours entered OR be marked as "Did Not Work"
- You cannot submit a timesheet for a week that already has one
- A digital signature is required before submission

## Saving Drafts

Click **Save Draft** at any time to save your progress without submitting. You can return later to complete and submit.

## DigiDocs benefit

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

## Example

For example, a submitted weekly timesheet can be reviewed in Approvals and then exported for payroll without rebuilding a spreadsheet.

articles.title: How do I edit a timesheet?
articles.summary: Instructions for editing draft or rejected timesheets.
articles.content_md:
# How do I edit a timesheet?

## When Can I Edit?

You can only edit timesheets with these statuses:
- **Draft** - Not yet submitted
- **Rejected** - Returned by manager with comments

Approved or processed timesheets cannot be edited.

## How to Edit

1. Go to **Timesheets**
2. Find the timesheet in the list
3. Click on it to open
4. You'll be taken to the edit form automatically
5. Make your changes
6. Click **Save** or **Submit**

## After Rejection

If your timesheet was rejected:
1. Read the manager's comments (shown at the top or indicated by "See manager comments")
2. Make the requested corrections
3. Update your signature if needed
4. Re-submit the timesheet

## DigiDocs benefit

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

## Example

For example, a submitted weekly timesheet can be reviewed in Approvals and then exported for payroll without rebuilding a spreadsheet.

articles.title: What do timesheet statuses mean?
articles.summary: Explanation of Draft, Submitted, Approved, Rejected, Processed, and Adjusted statuses.
articles.content_md:
# What do timesheet statuses mean?

## Draft

Timesheet is saved but not submitted. Only you can see it. You can continue editing.

## Submitted (shows as "Pending")

Timesheet has been submitted for manager approval. You cannot edit until reviewed. Shows an amber clock icon.

## Approved

Manager has approved the timesheet. Shows a green checkmark. It will be processed for payroll.

## Rejected

Manager has returned the timesheet with comments. Shows a red X. Read the feedback, make corrections, and re-submit.

## Processed

Timesheet has been processed for payroll. Shows a blue package icon. This is the final status for normal timesheets.

## Adjusted

The timesheet was adjusted after approval (rare case for corrections). Shows an amber warning icon.

## DigiDocs benefit

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

## Example

For example, a submitted weekly timesheet can be reviewed in Approvals and then exported for payroll without rebuilding a spreadsheet.

articles.title: Can I delete a timesheet?
articles.summary: Information about deleting timesheets.
articles.content_md:
# Can I delete a timesheet?

## For Employees

Employees cannot delete timesheets. If you have a draft you no longer need, you can leave it and create a new one for a different week.

## For Managers

Managers can delete any timesheet:

1. Go to **Timesheets**
2. Find the timesheet in the list
3. Click the red **trash** icon
4. Confirm the deletion

**Warning:** Deleting a timesheet permanently removes it and all its entries. This cannot be undone.

## DigiDocs benefit

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

## Example

articles.title: How do I perform a van daily check?
articles.summary: Step-by-step guide to completing a van daily check.
articles.content_md:
# How do I perform a van daily check?

## Quick steps

1. Go to **Van Daily Checks**.
2. Click **New Daily Check**.
3. Select the van and week ending date.
4. Enter the current mileage.
5. Complete each daily checklist tab.
6. Add required comments for any failed item.
7. Save a draft or submit the check.

## Checklist rules

Van daily checks use the van checklist for items such as fluids, tyres, lights, mirrors, brakes, body condition, seat belt, and steering.

If you mark an item as failed, add a clear defect comment before submitting. Defects can create workshop follow-up tasks for managers to review.

## DigiDocs benefit

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

## Example

For example, a van defect recorded during a daily check can be followed up through Workshop Tasks and later reviewed in reports.

articles.title: What happens when I report a van defect?
articles.summary: How failed van check items become trackable workshop work.
articles.content_md:
# What happens when I report a van defect?

When you mark a van daily check item as **Fail**, the app records the defect with your comments.

## What managers see

Defects can be reviewed in daily check reports and workshop workflows. If a repair is needed, a linked Workshop Task can be created or updated so the issue is tracked through to completion.

## What to include

Add practical detail, such as:

- What is wrong
- Which side or part is affected
- Whether the vehicle can still be used safely
- Any temporary action already taken

Clear comments help the workshop and managers resolve the defect faster.

## DigiDocs benefit

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

## Example

For example, a van defect recorded during a daily check can be followed up through Workshop Tasks and later reviewed in reports.

The van daily check covers the main safety and condition items needed before use.

## Typical van checklist

1. Oil, fuel, coolant levels, and leaks
2. Wheels and nuts
3. Tyres
4. Windows and wipers
5. Mirrors
6. Visual body condition
7. Lights and flashing beacons
8. Instrument gauges and horn
9. Seat belt
10. Visual interior condition
11. Locking devices
12. Steering
13. Parking brake
14. Brake test

Plant and HGV checks now have their own modules and FAQ categories because their forms and compliance needs are different.

## DigiDocs benefit

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

articles.summary: Understanding duplicate daily check prevention.
articles.content_md:
# Why can I not create a van daily check?

The app prevents duplicate daily checks for the same van and reporting period.

If you see a duplicate warning:

- Check whether a draft already exists.
- Confirm you selected the correct van.
- Confirm the date or week ending is correct.
- Open the existing check instead of creating a second one.

This prevents conflicting records for the same asset and period.

## DigiDocs benefit

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

articles.title: How do I request annual leave?
articles.summary: Instructions for submitting an annual leave request.
articles.content_md:
# How do I request annual leave?

## Summary Card

At the top of the Absence page, you'll see your annual leave summary for the current UK financial year (April to April):
- **Total Allowance** - Your yearly entitlement
- **Approved Taken** - Days already used
- **Pending** - Days in pending requests
- **Remaining** - Days still available

## Making a Request

1. Go to **Absence** from the dashboard
2. Click **Request Annual Leave** to expand the form
3. Fill in:
   - **Start Date** - First day of leave
   - **End Date** - Last day (optional for single-day requests)
   - **Half Day** - Check if taking only half a day (AM or PM)
   - **Notes** - Any additional information
4. Review the **Request Summary** showing calculated days
5. Click **Submit Request**

## Half-Day Requests

For half-days:
1. Check the **Half Day** box
2. Select **AM** or **PM**
3. End date is automatically disabled (half-days are single-day only)

## Allowance Check

The system prevents over-booking:
- **Projected Remaining** shows what's left after this request
- If negative, you'll see a warning and can't submit

## DigiDocs benefit

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

articles.summary: Changing task status and adding completion details.
articles.content_md:
# How do I update a workshop task status?

## Status Flow

Tasks follow this workflow:

```
Pending → Logged → In Progress → Completed
              ↓           ↓
          On Hold ←───────┘
```

## Status Actions

Click on a task to open it, then use the action buttons:

### From Pending

- **Mark In Progress** - Opens dialog for a short comment (e.g., "Parts on order")
- **Mark Complete** - Jump straight to completion

### From In Progress

- **Put On Hold** - Pause the task (e.g., waiting for parts)
- **Mark Complete** - Finish the task

### From On Hold

- **Resume** - Continue working (returns to In Progress)
- **Mark Complete** - Finish the task

## Marking Complete

When completing a task:

1. Click **Mark Complete**
2. A dialog appears asking for:
   - **Completion Notes** - What was done
   - **Mileage** - Vehicle mileage at completion
   - **Category Updates** - Depending on category, update service/cambelt dates
3. Click **Complete Task**

## DigiDocs benefit

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

## Example

For example, a defect task can hold comments, attachments, actioned notes, and completion history.

# What are Toolbox Talks?

**Note:** Sending is for Managers and Admins only. All employees can receive.

## Purpose

Toolbox Talks are important safety messages that:
- Communicate critical safety information
- Require employee acknowledgment

- **Non-blocking** - Employees can dismiss after reading

# How do roles and permissions work?

DigiDocs uses role and team permissions to decide which modules a user can access.

## Module permissions

Each role or team setup can grant access to modules such as Timesheets, Daily Checks, Projects, Absence, Maintenance, Fleet, Workshop Tasks, Reports, Quotes, Inventory, and admin tools.

## Full access roles

Admin and super admin roles can have full access. View As and effective role behavior can change what a user sees while testing another role.

FAQ categories use the same module names, so restricted FAQ content is only returned to users with the matching module access.

# How do I manage roles? (Admin)

**Note:** This is for Admins only.

## Accessing Roles

1. Go to **Users**
2. Click the **Roles** tab

## Viewing Roles

See all roles with:
- Name and display name
- Description
- User count (how many users have this role)
- Permission count (modules enabled)

## Creating a New Role

1. Click **Add Role**
2. Enter:
   - **Name** (internal identifier, e.g., "employee-electrician")
   - **Display Name** (shown in UI, e.g., "Electrician")
   - **Description** (optional)
   - **Is Manager/Admin** - Toggle for full access
3. Save the role

## Editing Permissions

1. Click on a role row to expand
2. Toggle modules on/off using checkboxes:
   - ☑️ Enabled - User can access this module
   - ☐ Disabled - User cannot access
3. Changes save automatically

## Deleting Roles

A role can only be deleted if no users are assigned to it.

articles.summary: How category module gates control Help content.
articles.content_md:
# How does FAQ visibility work?

FAQ categories can have a module gate.

## Public categories

Categories with no module gate are visible to all signed-in users. Examples include Getting Started and Troubleshooting.

## Module-gated categories

Categories with a module gate are only returned by the FAQ API when the current effective role can access that module.

This means direct API calls and the Help page both use the same permission boundary for restricted FAQ content.

## DigiDocs benefit

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

## Example

For example, an admin can publish Inventory help only to users who have access to the Inventory module.

# The app won't load - what should I do?

## Basic Steps

1. **Check internet connection** - Ensure you have WiFi or mobile data
2. **Refresh the page** - Pull down to refresh (mobile) or press F5 (desktop)
3. **Clear browser cache** - In browser settings, clear cached data
4. **Try a different browser** - Chrome or Safari recommended

## PWA Issues

If using the installed app:
1. Delete the app from home screen
2. Clear browser cache
3. Revisit the website
4. Re-add to home screen

## Still Not Working?

Contact your administrator with:
- What you were trying to do
- Any error messages shown
- Your device type and browser

articles.summary: Troubleshooting data save issues.
articles.content_md:
# My data isn't saving - what's wrong?

## Check Connection

You need internet to save data. Check:
- WiFi or mobile data is active
- You're not in airplane mode
- The offline banner isn't showing

## Required Fields

Make sure all required fields are filled:
- Look for error messages in red
- Required fields are marked with *
- Defect comments are required when marking items as Failed

## Session Expired

If you've been idle too long:
- You may need to log in again
- Try refreshing and logging back in

## Validation Errors

- Week ending must be a Sunday
- Duplicate inspections for same vehicle/week aren't allowed
- All days must have entries or be marked "Did Not Work"

## Try Again

1. Refresh the page
2. Re-enter the data
3. Click save again
4. If still failing, note any error message and contact admin

This keeps the workflow consistent and auditable. Users get a focused screen for the job they need to do, while managers get structured information they can filter, approve, and report on.

Access Denied usually means your current effective role does not have permission for that module.
